hero.title: 5 Days — Danang, Ba Na Hills & UNESCO Hoi An.
hero.titleAccent: Heritage Train Over Hai Van Pass to Imperial Hue.
hero.intro: Central Vietnam by train — the way it was meant to be seen. The Heritage Train departs Danang at 07:50 and crosses over Hai Van Pass at 496 metres, with Lang Co Bay visible far below. Five hours later you are in Hue, the last imperial capital of Vietnam. Ba Na Hills with the Golden Bridge on day four. And between these: basket boat through the Hoi An coconut palm wetland, and a guided walk through the UNESCO Ancient Town — Hoi An's Japanese bridge, Chinese assembly halls, and tailor shops unchanged for 400 years.
hero.heroImageAlt: Heritage Train scenic Hai Van Pass Danang to Hue Vietnam

quickFacts.saving: Save RM547 per person (20% online savings)

Hoi An Ancient Town guided walk with entrance fees
Daily breakfast, lunches and dinners as specified
excludes: Personal cyclo tour in Hue (own expense)

overview.heading: 5 Days Danang + Ba Na Hills + Hoi An + Heritage Train to Hue
body: This five-day package takes the scenic train from Danang to Hue rather than the mountain road — a fundamentally different experience that uses the historic South China Sea railway route, climbing over Hai Van Pass with ocean panoramas that the road route does not provide. The Heritage Train (Reunification Express) at 07:50 from Danang is the same railway that carried Vietnamese traders and imperial passengers for over a century, passing through the Hai Van Tunnel and briefly stopping at Lang Co Station with views of the UNESCO-nominated lagoon.

The itinerary covers all four of Central Vietnam's essential experiences: Danang city tour on day one, the Heritage Train to Hue on day two with the Imperial Citadel and Khai Dinh Tomb, Hoi An basket boat and Ancient Town on day three, and Ba Na Hills with the Golden Bridge on day four. The routing returns to Danang as the base hotel, allowing a cohesive experience with the final day's beach or city options.

Ba Na Hills on day four is the modern counterpart to the colonial-era train: Sun World's 1,489-metre hilltop resort, with the Golden Bridge (the two giant stone hands), the French Village, the cable car records, and a full buffet lunch. Together, the train and Ba Na Hills give this package a distinctively varied transport and landscape experience compared to the Hai Van Pass road route.

highlights.heading: Why This 5-Day Danang Heritage Train Package Stands Apart

items.title: Heritage Train — Danang to Hue Over Hai Van Pass
items.body: The train from Danang to Hue departs at 07:50 and arrives at 10:35, travelling a route that hugs the South China Sea coast, climbs over the Hai Van Pass at 496 metres, and passes Lang Co Bay before descending into Hue. The views from the train window as it rounds the headland above Lang Co Bay — the lagoon on one side, the sea on the other — are considered among the most spectacular train views in Asia. This is the Reunification Express line, the railway that links Hanoi to Ho Chi Minh City and has operated since 1936.
items.imageAlt: Heritage Train Vietnam Danang Hue Hai Van Pass scenic railway

items.title: Hue Imperial City — Vietnam's Last Dynasty
items.body: Hue was the capital of the Nguyen Dynasty from 1802 to 1945. The Imperial Citadel (Dai Noi) is a 520-hectare walled complex with the Forbidden Purple City at its centre — a Vietnamese adaptation of Beijing's Forbidden City, largely destroyed in the 1968 Tet Offensive but extensively restored. The guided visit covers the Ngo Mon Gate, Thai Hoa Palace, and the restored sections of the royal compound. After the citadel, Khai Dinh Royal Tomb (1920–1931) is the most dramatic of Hue's imperial mausoleums — built in French-influenced style with an extraordinary mosaic interior.
items.imageAlt: Hue Imperial Citadel Nguyen Dynasty Vietnam Forbidden City

items.title: Basket Boat & Hoi An Ancient Town
items.body: Day three combines the Cam Thanh basket boat experience (coracle ride through the Bay Mau coconut palm wetland, with local fishermen demonstrating net casting, crab fishing, boat spinning, and singing) with the UNESCO Hoi An Ancient Town guided walk. The Japanese Covered Bridge (1593), Tan Ky Old House, Fujian Assembly Hall (1757), and Museum of Trade Ceramics are all included. Free time for Hoi An's tailor shops — the best in Vietnam for both speed and quality of hand-sewn garments.
items.imageAlt: Basket boat coracle Hoi An Ancient Town UNESCO Vietnam

items.title: Ba Na Hills — The Golden Bridge
items.body: The Golden Bridge at Ba Na Hills — two giant stone hands reaching up from the mountainside to hold a golden-railed footbridge — is among the most photographed structures in Vietnam. The cable car ascent covers 5,801 metres and holds multiple world records (length, height change, longest non-stop). The hilltop French Village recreates a medieval European mountain town, with a château, bell tower, wine cellar, and cobblestone streets. The full Sun World combo (cable car + funicular + all attractions + buffet lunch) is included.
items.imageAlt: Ba Na Hills Golden Bridge Sun World Danang Vietnam France Village

itinerary.heading: Your 5-Day Danang Heritage Train to Hue Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Danang — City Tour
days.description: Arrive Da Nang International Airport, met by guide and transferred to hotel. Danang city tour: Marble Mountains (five limestone hills with Buddhist cave shrines and hilltop panoramic views), Son Tra Peninsula and Linh Ung Pagoda (67m Lady Buddha statue overlooking the sea), Danang Pink Church (Giac Vien Catholic Church, distinctive pink facade). My Quang noodle lunch — Danang's signature dish with turmeric, shrimp, and herbs. Evening seafood dinner on the Han River waterfront.
suggestedActivities: Son Tra Peninsula and Linh Ung Pagoda (Lady Buddha 67m)

days.title: Heritage Train Danang → Hue — Imperial City
days.description: 07:50: Board Heritage Train at Da Nang Station. The train climbs over Hai Van Pass (496m) with ocean views, passes Lang Co Bay, and arrives Hue at 10:35 (2 hours 45 minutes). Guide meets guests at Hue Station. City tour: Thien Mu Pagoda (seven-storey Phuoc Duyen tower, 1601, on the Perfume River bank), Thuy Xuan Incense Village (traditional cone-shaped incense production, vivid colours). Lunch. Optional cyclo tour from Hien Nhon Gate to Ngo Mon Gate (own expense, approximately 30 minutes). Guided tour of Hue Imperial Citadel: Ngo Mon Gate, Thai Hoa Palace, Forbidden Purple City ruins. Khai Dinh Royal Tomb (French-influenced mausoleum, 1920–1931, extraordinary mosaic interior). Hotel check-in Hue. Dinner.
suggestedActivities: Hai Van Pass mountain view from train
Lang Co Bay visible from train
Thien Mu Pagoda (7-storey Phuoc Duyen tower)

days.title: Hoi An — Basket Boat & Ancient Town
days.description: Morning: Transfer Hue to Hoi An (90 km, approximately 2 hours). Hotel check-in Hoi An. Cam Thanh Basket Boat: coracle ride through Bay Mau coconut palm wetland with local fishermen demonstrating net casting, crab fishing, boat spinning, and karaoke singing on the water. Lunch. 13:30: Guided Hoi An Ancient Town walk with entrance fees: Japanese Covered Bridge (1593), Tan Ky Old House (200-year-old Chinese-Japanese merchant residence), Fujian Assembly Hall (1757), Museum of Trade Ceramics. Free time for tailors, lanterns, and market. Return to Danang (30 km, 45 minutes). Han Market / Vincom shopping. Dragon Bridge show (Fri, Sat, Sun at 9pm). BBQ hotpot dinner.
suggestedActivities: Transfer Hue to Hoi An (2 hrs)
Fishermen net casting and crab fishing
Boat spinning and karaoke on water
Hoi An Ancient Town guided walk
Free time for tailors and shopping

days.title: Ba Na Hills — Sun World Full Day
days.description: Morning departure for Ba Na Hills (45 km from Danang, approximately 45 minutes drive). Sun World Ba Na Hills full combo included: cable car ascent (5,801m, world record for longest non-stop), all rides and attractions (Golden Bridge, French Village, Fantasy Park, Le Jardin D'Amour gardens, wax museum), funicular to the summit, buffet lunch. The Golden Bridge held by two giant stone hands is the signature attraction. French Village recreates a medieval European hilltop resort at 1,489 metres elevation with 20°C weather even in summer. Return to Danang approximately 17:00. Han Market / Vincom shopping. Dinner.
suggestedActivities: Cable car ascent to Ba Na Hills (5,801m, world record)

days.title: Danang Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Danang. Options: My Khe Beach (30 km stretch, one of Asia's most beautiful), Museum of Cham Sculpture (world's largest collection of Cham Hindu-Buddhist art, 4th–15th centuries), or Danang city stroll. Transfer to Da Nang International Airport per agreed schedule.
suggestedActivities: Optional: My Khe Beach final swim

whyBook.heading: Central Vietnam by Train — Arranged by Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: The Heritage Train from Danang to Hue is the most scenic way to move between these two Central Vietnam cities. Dhesu includes the Heritage Train tickets within the package — the same scenic railway line that has connected Vietnam's two greatest imperial cities since 1936. Our Central Vietnam guides are experienced with both the train logistics and every site along the way.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Central Vietnam train packages are one of our most distinctive offerings — Heritage Train tickets are pre-booked and confirmed before your departure.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been arranging Central Vietnam journeys for Malaysian travellers. Our Danang, Hue, and Hoi An guides are seasoned and reliable.

points.title: Heritage Train + Ba Na Hills Included
points.body: Heritage Train tickets (Danang to Hue, 07:50–10:35) and the full Sun World Ba Na Hills combo (cable car + funicular + all attractions + buffet lunch) are both included in the package.

points.title: Three UNESCO Destinations in Five Days
points.body: Hue (UNESCO World Heritage Imperial City) and Hoi An (UNESCO World Heritage Ancient Town) with one night in each, plus the iconic Ba Na Hills — Central Vietnam's three headline experiences in a single package.

sections.heading: 5 Day Danang Heritage Train Hue Hoi An Ba Na Hills Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
The 5-day Danang Heritage Train to Hue package is the most scenic Central Vietnam tour available from Malaysia. Taking the Reunification Express train from Danang over Hai Van Pass to Hue is a quintessentially Vietnamese experience — the same coastal railway route that has operated since the colonial era, passing coastline and mountain pass views that make the 2-hour 45-minute journey as memorable as the destinations themselves.

This package covers three of Central Vietnam's four UNESCO World Heritage experiences: Hue's Imperial Citadel and Royal Tombs, Hoi An's Ancient Town (UNESCO 1999), and the journey itself on the historic South China Sea railway. Ba Na Hills adds the modern counterpoint — the Golden Bridge, the French Village, and the world-record cable car.

faq.heading: Questions About the 5-Day Danang Heritage Train Package

items.question: Is the Heritage Train comfortable?
items.answer: Yes. The air-conditioned Heritage Train from Danang to Hue is a comfortable journey in reserved seats. The 2-hour 45-minute trip (07:50–10:35) has large windows for the Hai Van Pass views. Tickets are pre-booked and confirmed. Your guide meets you at Hue Station on arrival.

items.question: How does this differ from the 5-day Danang Hue Hoi An package (pkgid 2091)?
items.answer: The main difference is transport to Hue: this package takes the Heritage Train (07:50–10:35, scenic coastal route over Hai Van Pass by rail), while the other package (pkgid 2091) drives over Hai Van Pass by road. Both cover the same sites in Hue and Hoi An. The train gives a different perspective — coastal and sea views from the rail line rather than the mountain road. Choose based on your preference for the journey experience.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Arrive at Da Nang International Airport with your Malaysian passport.

items.question: Can I see the Dragon Bridge fire show during this tour?
items.answer: Yes, if your Hoi An day (Day 3) falls on a Friday, Saturday, or Sunday — the Dragon Bridge fire and water show runs only on those three evenings at 9pm. Your guide will advise based on your departure date.
